name: csharp-pro
description: Write modern C# code with advanced features like records, pattern
capabilities: Generate complex C# code patterns including SOLID design principles
Explain advanced C# concepts like async/await, memory management, and performance optimization
Provide architectural guidance for enterprise-level applications
Help debug and refactor existing C# codebases
Generate unit tests and integration tests following best practices
Guide through .NET ecosystem tools and libraries
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The static analyzer scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. Risk score is 0/100, indicating no detected threats. The skill provides C# programming guidance through prompt templates without any filesystem, network, or script execution capabilities.
valueStatement: This skill helps advanced C# developers tackle complex programming challenges, design patterns, and architectural decisions with expert guidance and code examples.
limitations: Does not execute or compile C# code - only provides guidance and examples
Cannot access your local project files without explicit file paths
May not have knowledge of very new .NET 9+ features released after training data
Does not provide debugging for runtime errors in live applications

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers and architects building large-scale systems
useCases.title: Enterprise Application Architecture
useCases.description: Design scalable and maintainable C# applications using domain-driven design, microservices patterns, and clean architecture principles.

useCases.title: Code Review and Refactoring
useCases.description: Get expert feedback on existing C# code and receive suggestions for improving performance, readability, and adherence to best practices.

useCases.targetUser: Intermediate to advanced C# developers expanding their skills
useCases.title: Learning Advanced Concepts
useCases.description: Understand complex topics like memory management, concurrency patterns, and advanced LINQ operations with detailed explanations and examples.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a C# class that implements [DESCRIBE FUNCTIONALITY] using best practices and proper error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate simple to moderate complexity C# code with proper structure

promptTemplates.title: Design Pattern Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the [PATTERN NAME] design pattern in C# for [USE CASE]. Include explanation of when to use this pattern and potential drawbacks.
promptTemplates.scenario: Apply established design patterns to solve architectural problems

promptTemplates.title: Performance Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this C# code for performance issues: [PASTE CODE]. Suggest optimizations for memory usage and execution speed.
promptTemplates.scenario: Identify and fix performance bottlenecks in existing code

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this proposed architecture [DESCRIBE ARCHITECTURE]. What are the pros and cons? How would you improve scalability and maintainability?
promptTemplates.scenario: Get expert feedback on application architecture decisions

bestPractices: Always specify async method names with Async suffix for clarity
Use dependency injection for testability and loose coupling
Prefer composition over inheritance in C# design
Leverage interfaces for abstraction and mockability
antiPatterns: Avoid catching generic Exception without proper handling or logging
Do not use async void except for event handlers
Avoid blocking on async code with .Wait() or .Result
Do not expose mutable collections as public properties

faq.question: Can this skill compile and run my C# code?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides code examples and guidance but cannot execute or compile C# code. Use an IDE or dotnet CLI for running code.

faq.question: Which .NET versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers .NET Framework, .NET Core, and .NET 5+ with version-specific guidance where differences exist.

faq.question: Can this help with C# interview preparation?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can explain concepts, generate practice problems, and review your answers for technical interviews.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Unity or game development?
faq.answer: The skill covers general C# patterns which apply to Unity, but does not have specific Unity API knowledge.

faq.question: Can I use this for ASP.NET Core development?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers web frameworks including ASP.NET Core, MVC, Razor Pages, and minimal APIs.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub Copilot?
faq.answer: This skill provides deeper explanatory guidance and architectural advice, while Copilot focuses on code completion. They complement each other well.
